content:
hi, I was trying to set up the rust for linux experiment, and while I thought everything was complete, using make LLVM=1 rustavailable validation, I ran into the following issues:

lwz@lwz-virtual-machine:~/linux$ make LLVM=1 rustavailable

*** libclang (used by the Rust bindings generator 'bindgen')
*** version does not match Clang's. This may be a problem.
*** libclang version: 18.1.2
*** Clang version: 14.0.0

Rust is available!

It's complaining about a version mismatch, but it already shows rust available, is that a problem? And the rust support option didn't come up when trying the general setup.
When installing clang and libclang, I direct reference jackos tutorial (https://www.jackos.io/rust-kernel/rust-for-linux.html#dependencies), use the following command:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y bc bison curl clang fish flex git gcc libclang-dev libelf-dev lld llvm-dev libncurses-dev make neovim qemu-system-x86
In menu config , type / and search for RUST . It’ll show you what the dependent options are and their current values. I often found that having loadable modules = Y prevents rust from being enabled.
i had a similar issue... had to resolve config dependencies. in my case i had to disable module versioning. ./scripts/config -d MODVERSIONS

you might also want to try

./scripts/config -d RANDSTRUCT VERSIONS
./scripts/config -d GCC_PLUGINS
./scripts/config -d DEBUG_INFO_BTF

Aswin Unnikrishnan said:

In menu config , type / and search for RUST . It’ll show you what the dependent options are and their current values. I often found that having loadable modules = Y prevents rust from being enabled.

Thanks! The problem is having loadable modules=Y. Now everything is Ok!
The environment is now set up, showing rust is available. I've also enabled rust modules in make menuconfig (which prints some information), but how do I then verify that these modules actually work?
If you’re talking about the sample rust modules, You build your kernel and load it into a VM and see if it prints something to dmesg during the boot

i found an guidence in the YouTube( **https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tPs1uRqOnlk**) and set up environment successfully. However it can only success on the rust branche, there will be some problem in the rust-next branche(when run “qemu-system-x86_64 -nographic -kernel vmlinux -initrd ../busybox/ramdisk.img” qemu kernel will panic). But anyway, follow the instruction the environment has been set up successfully, i can work on the rust branche.
One difference is that the rust-next branch doesn't have qemu-busybox-min.config
